title: pinto beans
ingredients: 1 lb. dry pinto beans; soaked overnight, drained, and rinsed
4 1/2 cups water; enough to cover beans by about 2/3"
instructions: Set the Instant Pot to Sauté mode. Heat olive oil; add onion and garlic and cook until soft and fragrant, 3–4 minutes. Ensure no browned bits are stuck to the bottom of the pot.
Turn off Sauté mode. Add the drained beans, salt, MSG, bay leaves and cumin to the pot.
Pour in water until it sits about an inch above the top of the beans. Stir gently to combine.
Cook on High Pressure for 9 minutes. Allow the pot to undergo a natural pressure release for 15 minutes before venting any remaining steam.
Remove the lid and stir in 1 Tbsp of the aged sherry vinegar. Taste, and add more vinegar or extra salt if needed.
If a thicker glaze is desired, turn on Sauté mode for 2–3 minutes to reduce the remaining liquid.
notes: Adding the vinegar at the very end is crucial; pressure cooking the acid would make the bean skins tough. Measuring water by height (2/3" above the beans) ensures the perfect ratio regardless of exact bean volume.
